From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Royal anthem of Sweden

Not to be confused with [Kongesangen](/wiki/Kongesangen "Kongesangen").

Kungssången

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| The original music for male chorus by Otto Lindblad. | |
| ---  Royal anthem of [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden")  --- | |
| Lyrics | [Carl Vilhelm August Strandberg](/wiki/C.V.A._Strandberg "C.V.A. Strandberg") |
| Music | [Otto Lindblad](/wiki/Otto_Lindblad "Otto Lindblad") |
| Adopted | 1844 |
|  | |
| Audio sample | |
| * [file](/wiki/File:Kungss%C3%A5ngen_ZS_V78-0021.wav "File:Kungssången ZS V78-0021.wav") * [help](/wiki/Help:Media "Help:Media") | |
|  | |

***Kungssången***[[1]](#cite_note-1) (*The King's Song*) is the [Swedish](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden") [royal anthem](/wiki/Royal_anthem "Royal anthem"). It is also known by its first line, ***Ur svenska hjärtans djup en gång*** (*English: Once from the depths of Swedish hearts*). Although sung on such occasions as the [King's birthday](/wiki/Carl_XVI_Gustaf_of_Sweden "Carl XVI Gustaf of Sweden"), the annual opening of the [Riksdag](/wiki/Riksdag "Riksdag") and the [Nobel Prize](/wiki/Nobel_Prize "Nobel Prize") ceremony, the song is not considered the [Swedish national anthem](/wiki/Swedish_national_anthem "Swedish national anthem"). *[Du gamla, Du fria](/wiki/Du_gamla,_Du_fria "Du gamla, Du fria")* is the *de facto* national anthem of Sweden, but has never been officially recognised.

The lyrics were written by [Carl Vilhelm August Strandberg](/wiki/C.V.A._Strandberg "C.V.A. Strandberg") and the music composed for four part male chorus by [Otto Lindblad](/wiki/Otto_Lindblad "Otto Lindblad"). *Kungssången* replaced the previous royal anthem, *[Bevare Gud vår kung](/wiki/Bevare_Gud_v%C3%A5r_kung "Bevare Gud vår kung")*, which was sung to the melody of the British royal anthem, *[God Save the King](/wiki/God_Save_the_King "God Save the King")*. It was first performed in [Lund](/wiki/Lund "Lund") on 5 December 1844 at a party arranged by the [University](/wiki/Lund_University "Lund University") to celebrate the accession of King [Oscar I](/wiki/Oscar_I_of_Sweden "Oscar I of Sweden"), and was adopted as the royal anthem in 1893.

## Lyrics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kungss%C3%A5ngen&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Lyrics")]

Only the first and fifth stanzas are normally sung (and only the first if the [King](/wiki/Monarchy_of_Sweden "Monarchy of Sweden") is present). The other three are very rarely ever heard.
